description: Notes from the human editor for the decider to factor into triage and patching. Read at the start of every run. Mark notes as consumed after acting on them.

notes.subject: CAT-005 approved pending proposal — route analyst output to curmudgeon before prose
notes.note: Steve approved CAT-005 (V51.1 Refraction Cascade) in principle. Structural decision: new section under The Model tab (option a). Also requested a new code_analysis sub-category tag (boolean, analogous to relabels_standard/post_hoc/derives_from_dome) marking WINs whose verdict is flipped by the refraction cascade. Analyst has been assigned EXP-050 to write a RECOMMENDED PROPOSAL PACKAGE — NOT section prose. When EXP-050 lands, decider must (a) NOT integrate it as prose (replacement_html will be null); (b) create a curmudgeon tracker entry at priority-new for the proposal package; (c) flag ISS-696 to block on curmudgeon review of EXP-050; (d) only after curmudgeon signs off on the package, create a follow-up EXP-NNN for analyst to write the actual section content. The entire point of this two-phase workflow is to lock structural decisions (new section placement, new tag naming/semantics, which WIN verdicts flip) before spending tokens on prose. See monitor/analyst/category-proposals/CAT-005.json for the full human decision block.
